prs.title: feat: add metadata fallback for TslibDataModule and fix multivariate …
prs.body:
# [ENH] Add metadata fallback for TslibDataModule and fix multivariate target handling

#### Reference Issues/PRs
Fixes #2075.

This PR introduces two main improvements to the experimental [TslibDataModule](cci:2://file:///Users/saadarqam/pytorch-forecasting/pytorch_forecasting/data/_tslib_data_module.py:234:0-939:31):

1.  **Metadata Fallback Inference**: Implemented [_infer_metadata](cci:1://file:///Users/saadarqam/pytorch-forecasting/pytorch_forecasting/data/_tslib_data_module.py:377:4-412:23) in [TslibDataModule](cci:2://file:///Users/saadarqam/pytorch-forecasting/pytorch_forecasting/data/_tslib_data_module.py:234:0-939:31). Previously, the module strictly required the underlying dataset to have a [get_metadata()](cci:1://file:///Users/saadarqam/pytorch-forecasting/pytorch_forecasting/data/timeseries/_timeseries_v2.py:321:4-332:28) method. It now attempts to infer the necessary column mappings (targets, features, and types) from common attributes found in generic PyTorch datasets (e.g., `.target`, `.feature_cols`, `.cat`, `.num`). This makes the [TslibDataModule](cci:2://file:///Users/saadarqam/pytorch-forecasting/pytorch_forecasting/data/_tslib_data_module.py:234:0-939:31) much more flexible for custom user-provided datasets.
2.  **Multivariate Target Fix**: Fixed a bug in `_TslibDataset.__getitem__` where multivariate targets were being returned as a list of tensors. Returning a list caused an `AttributeError` during tensor operations in the training pipeline. It now returns a single merged tensor of shape [(prediction_length, n_targets)](cci:1://file:///Users/saadarqam/pytorch-forecasting/pytorch_forecasting/data/_tslib_data_module.py:721:4-807:13).

prs.title: Feat/hide arrow tooltip
prs.body:
### feat(tooltip): add `hideArrow` prop to `TooltipContent`

### Description
This pull request adds an optional **`hideArrow`** prop to the `TooltipContent` component, allowing developers to hide the tooltip arrow when needed.  
This enhances flexibility for minimalist layouts, prevents visual overlap with surrounding UI elements, and aligns the Tooltip API with capabilities found in other UI libraries.

---

### Changes Made
- Extended `TooltipContentProps` to include a new optional boolean prop **`hideArrow`**.
- Updated `TooltipContent` to accept the `hideArrow` prop with a default value of **`false`** to maintain backward compatibility.
- Updated `sideOffset` behavior:
  - If `hideArrow` is `true` and no custom `sideOffset` is provided, default becomes **4**.
  - If the arrow is visible, default remains **0**.
  - Custom `sideOffset` values always take precedence.
- Conditionally render `TooltipPrimitive.Arrow` only when `hideArrow` is **false**.
- Ensured that all existing tooltip functionality, styling, and behaviors remain intact.
- Verified that the registry builds successfully (`pnpm registry:build`) and the component works across all style variants.

---

### Motivation
- Support cleaner, minimalist UI designs.
- Prevent tooltip arrows from overlapping adjacent UI components.
- Provide greater control to developers and designers.
- Align with similar features available in Radix, Material UI, and other popular UI libraries.

---

### Related Issue
- **#8769**

---

### Testing Performed
- Verified default tooltip behavior (arrow visible).
- Verified behavior when `hideArrow` is enabled (arrow hidden).
- Verified behavior with custom `sideOffset`.
- Confirmed rendering and animations across all tooltip sides.
- No regressions observed in existing tooltip usage.

prs.title: chore(ci): auto-comment on new issues with welcome + Discord link
prs.body:
# GitHub Actions: Auto-Reply to New Issues

## Description
Adds a GitHub Actions workflow to automatically comment on newly opened issues with a welcome message and a Discord invite link.

## File
`.github/workflows/issue-auto-reply.yml`

## Trigger
```yaml
on:
  issues:
    types: [opened]

permissions:
  issues: write
Thank you for raising this issue! Our team will review it soon.
For more queries or discussions or approval of requests, please join the Discord server for further discussion: Discord Link

prs.title: fixed the flickering issue ion loading issue#2
prs.body:
# 🐛 Fix Form Flickering on Startup

## Description
When running the project in Visual Studio, the first form flickers briefly while opening. This creates a poor user experience and makes the UI look less professional.

## Root Cause Analysis
The flickering was caused by multiple factors:

1. **Heavy Constructor Initialization**: Both `L_or_R.cs` and `Dashboard.cs` performed heavy operations during form construction
2. **DWM API Calls in Constructor**: `DwmSetWindowAttribute` calls during form creation caused visual updates
3. **Redundant Double Buffering**: Multiple `DoubleBuffered = true` settings in designer files
4. **Complex Form Transitions**: Login-to-dashboard transition created additional flickering
5. **Background Image Processing**: Large background images caused rendering delays

## Solution Implemented

### ✅ **Form Initialization Optimization**
- Moved heavy operations from constructors to `Load` events
- Implemented `SuspendLayout()`/`ResumeLayout()` pattern
- Added advanced double buffering with `SetStyle()` method

### ✅ **DWM API Optimization**
- Deferred `DwmSetWindowAttribute` calls to `Load` events
- Added error handling for older systems without DWM support
- Prevented visual updates during form construction

### ✅ **Memory Management**
- Optimized image loading with proper `using` statements
- Added graceful error handling for image processing
- Improved resource disposal

### ✅ **Designer File Cleanup**
- Removed redundant `DoubleBuffered = true` settings
- Centralized double buffering in constructors for better control

## Files Modified
- `Program.cs` - Simplified startup sequence with error handling
- `L or R.cs` - Optimized login form initialization
- `L or R.Designer.cs` - Removed redundant settings
- `Dashboard.cs` - Optimized dashboard initialization  
- `Dashboard.Designer.cs` - Removed redundant settings

## Technical Improvements
- **Advanced Double Buffering**: `ControlStyles.AllPaintingInWmPaint | ControlStyles.UserPaint | ControlStyles.DoubleBuffer | ControlStyles.ResizeRedraw`
- **Deferred Initialization**: Heavy operations moved to `Load` events
- **Error Handling**: Graceful handling of DWM API failures
- **Layout Optimization**: Proper use of `SuspendLayout()`/`ResumeLayout()`

## Expected Results
- ✅ No form flickering during startup
- ✅ Smooth form transitions
- ✅ Better performance with optimized initialization
- ✅ Professional user experience
- ✅ Improved visual stability

## Testing
- [x] No compilation errors
- [x] Forms initialize without flickering
- [x] Smooth login-to-dashboard transition
- [x] Graceful error handling on older systems
- [x] Maintained all existing functionality

## Impact
This fix significantly improves the user experience by eliminating visual flickering during application startup and form transitions, making the Student Management System appear more professional and polished.

prs.title: fixed the resume template layout
prs.body:
### PR Title
Fix: Resume Builder sidebar profile pocket overlapping preview on tablets

### Summary
- Adjust sidebar profile pocket to be sticky only on large screens.
- Lower its stacking context to prevent overlap with the preview.
- Improves alignment and spacing between the sidebar and preview on tablets.

### Changes
- `app/builder/[id]/page.tsx`
  - Update wrapper classes: `sticky z-30` → `relative lg:sticky z-10`.

### Before
- Profile pocket was always sticky with high z-index, wedging between sidebar and preview on tablets.

### After
- Small/medium screens: pocket is non-sticky and no longer overlaps the preview.
- Large screens (`lg+`): pocket remains sticky at the bottom without obstructing content.

### Screenshots
- Please attach “Before” and “After” screenshots demonstrating the tablet layout.

### Testing
- Viewport 768–1024px: verify pocket is not sticky and does not overlap.
- Viewport ≥1024px: verify pocket is sticky and preview remains unobstructed.
- Toggle sidebar open/closed to confirm no layout shifts.

### Risks/Notes
- Low risk; Tailwind class changes only, no logic changes.

### Related Issue
- Closes #83

prs.title: chore: downgrade to React 18 and fix compatibility with MUI 5
prs.body:
# Pull Request to Task Manager

Thank you for your contribution to our project! We greatly appreciate your effort and time. Rest assured, if all checks pass and the changes align with our project goals, your pull request will be reviewed promptly and merged.

## Table of Contents

- [Type of Changes](#type-of-changes)
- [Related Issues](#related-issues)
- [Checklist](#checklist)
- [Additional Notes](#additional-notes)
- [Thank You](#thank-you)

### Type of Changes
Please check the type of change your PR introduces:

- [ ] Feature Addition
- [x] Bugfix
- [ ] Documentation Update
- [ ] Refactoring
- [ ] Hotfix
- [ ] UI/UX Improvement
- [ ] Other (please describe)

---

### Bugfix
1. **Bug Description**: The project was breaking due to incompatible React version (v19) with MUI, Emotion, and testing libraries.
2. **Steps to Reproduce**:
    - Clone the repo
    - Run `npm install` with React v19 installed
    - Run `npm start` and observe runtime errors (`ReactDOM.render is not a function`)
3. **Expected Behavior**: App should compile and render without errors.
4. **Actual Behavior**: App fails to run, showing runtime errors and peer dependency warnings.
5. **Fix Description**: 
    - Downgraded React and ReactDOM to the latest stable v18.
    - Updated MUI v5, @emotion/react, @emotion/styled, and @testing-library/react to compatible versions.
    - Updated `index.js` to use React 18 `createRoot` API:

```javascript
import React from 'react';
import ReactDOM from 'react-dom/client';
import { Provider } from 'react-redux';
import store from './redux/store';
import App from './App';

const root = ReactDOM.createRoot(document.getElementById('root'));
root.render(
  <Provider store={store}>
    <App />
  </Provider>
);

prs.title: Added solution for sliding window problem (Hacktoberfest 2025)
prs.body:
## 🤝 **Join the Discussion!**

Thank you for contributing! Before you submit this Pull Request, please take a moment to:

1. Follow the [maintainer](https://github.com/DhanushNehru) of the repository
2. Join ongoing discussions https://github.com/DhanushNehru/Hacktoberfest2024/discussions/405 and make sure you voted.

---

### 📋 **PR Details**

**Description of Changes:**  

# Longest Common Subsequence (LCS) – Python Solution

## 📝 Problem Statement
Given two strings `text1` and `text2`, return the **length of their longest common subsequence (LCS)**.  
A subsequence of a string is formed by deleting some (or no) characters without changing the order of the remaining characters.  

**Example:**  
- `"ace"` is a subsequence of `"abcde"`.  
- LCS of `"abcde"` and `"ace"` is `"ace"`, which has length `3`.

---

## 💡 Approach
This solution uses **Dynamic Programming**:

```python
# dp[i][j] stores the length of LCS of first i chars of text1 and first j chars of text2
if text1[i-1] == text2[j-1]:
    dp[i][j] = 1 + dp[i-1][j-1]
else:
    dp[i][j] = max(dp[i-1][j], dp[i][j-1])


### 📝 **Contributor Info**

**Your Name:**  
Saad Arqam

---

### ✅ **Checklist Before Submitting**

- [ ✅ ] I have checked the code for errors and ensured it follows the project's guidelines.
- [ ✅ ] I have read the contribution guidelines.
- [ ✅ ] I have participated in related discussions or created an issue if necessary.
- [ ✅ ] I have included my name above.

---

### 📣 **Additional Comments**

_This implementation is clean, easy to understand, and ready for educational purposes.
It also includes docstrings explaining the approach, inputs, and outputs._

---

Thank you for contributing! 🎉 Your participation helps make this project better. Let's grow together

###  Post Project Flow + Explore Integration

#### User Flow
- Users can post new projects with metadata (tech stack, tags, difficulty, visibility)
- Projects marked as:

  - **Invite Only** → visible only in "My Projects" for creator

- Tags
- Difficulty Level
- Filters work dynamically on the `/explore-projects` page

- All integrated end-to-end and fully working

prs.title: Add skill-based filtering to /api/users route
prs.body:
This update enhances the `/api/users` endpoint to support filtering based on technical skills provided via the `stack` query parameter.

- Parse comma-separated skill names from `?stack=` query param  
  Example: `/api/users?stack=react,node`
- Return users who have **at least one** of the specified skills
- Use Prisma relation filtering (`UserSkill` → `Skill`)
- (Optional) Add basic pagination support using `?page=` and `?limit=`

### 📦 Sample Request

issues.title: Setup Next.js Frontend for Iris Robotics Club
issues.body:
### 📌 Description  
We need to initialize a **Next.js (JavaScript)** frontend project for the Iris Robotics Club. This will serve as the foundation for building our web platform, including member profiles, projects, events, and collaboration features.  

---

### ✅ Tasks  
- [ ] Create a new Next.js project using `npx create-next-app@latest` (JavaScript, no TypeScript)  
- [ ] Set up **Tailwind CSS** for styling (`npx tailwindcss init -p`)  
- [ ] Configure project folder structure (`components`, `pages`, `styles`)  
- [ ] Add common dependencies if needed (e.g., `framer-motion` for animations, `shadcn/ui` for UI components)  
- [ ] Update `README.md` with setup and run instructions  
- [ ] Push the initialized project to the repository  

---

### 🎯 Expected Outcome  
A working **Next.js + Tailwind (JavaScript)** project committed to the repository, ready for further development of the Iris Robotics Club frontend.  

---
